questions.question: Explain the difference between AND nodes and OR nodes in an AND-OR graph. Provide an example illustrating problem decomposition.
keyPoints: AND nodes require ALL successors to be solved (problem decomposition)
OR nodes require only ONE successor to be solved (choice among alternatives)
Example such as Tower of Hanoi or theorem proving showing decomposition
Solution is a graph, not a single path

questions.question: Describe the AO* algorithm in detail. Explain how it computes costs for AND and OR nodes, how SOLVED status is propagated, and trace an example.
keyPoints: AO* initializes with start node and heuristic estimates
Iteratively selects and expands nodes from the best partial solution graph
AND node cost = sum of all [arc cost + h(successor)]
OR node cost = min of [arc cost + h(successor)]
AND node SOLVED when ALL successors SOLVED
OR node SOLVED when ANY successor SOLVED
Cost revisions propagate backward to ancestors
Terminates when start node is SOLVED or cost becomes infinity

questions.question: Compare AND search (AO*) with standard OR search (A*). When is AND search more appropriate?
keyPoints: A* finds a single path; AO* finds a solution graph
A* works on OR-only graphs; AO* handles AND-OR graphs
AND search is appropriate for problem decomposition scenarios
Applications: theorem proving, planning, game trees

questions.question: Explain problem reduction with reference to AND-OR search trees. Use the Tower of Hanoi as an example.
keyPoints: Problem reduction transforms a problem into easier subproblems
AND nodes decompose the problem; all subproblems must be solved
Tower of Hanoi: move N disks decomposes into move N-1 + move 1 + move N-1
Recursive decomposition until primitive (single disk) moves
